forecast-period.text: A high pressure system will move slowly eastwards across the Great Australian Bight during the next few days. The high will then move eastwards early next week to be centred southeast of Tasmania late Tuesday.
For latest warnings go to www.bom.gov.au, subscribe to RSS feeds, call 1300 659 210* or listen for warnings on relevant TV and radio broadcasts.
* Calls to 1300 numbers cost around 27.5c incl. GST, higher from mobiles or public phones.

Isolated showers about the agricultural area and West Coast district, scattered near southeastern and far western coasts. Possible small hail in the morning about the far southeast. Chance of a brief light shower in the far west. Maximum temperatures below average. Fresh southwest to southeasterly winds, gusty near southern coasts.
Isolated showers about the southern agricultural area and near western coasts. Maximum temperatures below average. Southwest to southeasterly winds, fresh at times near the coast.

Patchy morning fog about the South East and Murray districts. Isolated showers about the southern agricultural area and near far western coasts. Maximum temperatures below average. South to southeasterly winds.

text.p: Patchy morning fog in the west. Isolated showers about the southern agricultural area and near far western coasts. Maximum temperatures below average. South to southeasterly winds.

Cumulative rainfall totals to the end of Sunday: less than 2 mm across the northern agricultural area and far south of the pastoral districts, increasing to 2 to 5 mm across the southern agricultural area. Higher totals of 5 to 10 mm are possible across southward facing coasts and up to 15 mm about the Mount Lofty Ranges and far southeast.
